subject: DevOps
title: Pipeline Triggers
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Pipeline Triggers.

hook.text: Something has to start the pipeline. A trigger is the event that says: 'Go. Build this now.'

buildup.text: The most common trigger is a Git push. Push code, pipeline starts. Simple and automatic.

discovery.text: Other triggers exist: pull request opened, schedule (nightly builds), or manual button press for releases.

twist.text: Smart triggers save time. Only rebuild the frontend if frontend files changed. Path filters are powerful.

climax.text: Webhook triggers connect external systems. A new Docker image pushed? Trigger the deploy pipeline.

punchline.text: Push, schedule, or webhook. The trigger starts the journey.

quiz.question: What is the most common trigger for a CI/CD pipeline?
options: A Git push to the repository
A manual email to the operations team
